{"step_by_step_thinking": "The spinal nucleus of the trigeminal nerve is involved in processing sensory information from the face, including touch, pain, and temperature. However, proprioception is the sense of the position and movement of body parts, which is processed by the dorsal column-medial lemniscal pathway. Therefore, the spinal nucleus of the trigeminal nerve does not process proprioception.", "answer_choice": "D"}
